subcodes.title: Words spoken on 1912-12-18 in London to E.G. Browne
subcodes.first_line: I have previously written that, for as long as the government and the people are not mingled together like milk and honey, prosperity shall remain unattainable… You must strive to change the characters of the people
subcodes.first_line_orig: من بدایتاً نوشتم که تا دولت و ملّت مانند شهد و شیر ... باید کوشید تا اخلاق ملت تبدیل شود و استعداد مشروطه و امور سائره حاصل گردد
subcodes.subjects: @proofs of the Manifestations of God, @spiritual foundations of true knowledge, @critique of Western values; culture, @development of capacity and readiness
